title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jimmy Sycamore keeps watch at a junction in the west corridor near the rooms of Sir Stanley Digby and Terence O'Rourke. Sitting in the shadow of an oak cabinet with a Leopold automatic pistol, Jimmy experiences anxiety and dark thoughts regarding the deaths of Ronnie DeVere and Gerry Wade. He contemplates the motives behind the seven ticking clocks on the mantelpiece and questions whether Bondel Brent's stories about the Seven Clocks Club are fabricated. Around two o'clock, Jimmy hears creaking sounds from below. He descends to the library and finds it empty, but he discovers an unlocked middle window facing the terrace. Jimmy locks the library door, pockets the key, and waits by the open window.

Meanwhile, Bondel Brent decides to investigate the exterior of the house to supplement the efforts of Bill and Jimmy. She dresses in riding pants, rubber-soled shoes, and a dark sweater to facilitate climbing. At 1:30, she climbs down the ivy from her window and moves along the terrace toward the southern end. While navigating the shadows, she is intercepted by Inspector Batter. Batter, who is tall and well-built, claims he is checking for unauthorized visitors. He questions Bondel's presence and identifies that she has climbed out of a window. Although Bondel resents that his conspicuous presence might interfere with her plans, Batter remains on the terrace.
